app.subtitle: Unconscious pattern analysis
app.name: Ozero's Mirror
app.line1: No consolation — just an accurate reflection.
app.line2: Keep only the facts. The problem is usually smaller than the story.
app.tagline: Training to separate fact from delusion in your own record.

measure.guide1: No need to write well. It doesn't have to be sentences.
measure.guide2: Write whatever comes up. If swearing comes up, write the swearing.
measure.guide3: Don't edit. Don't censor. Pour it out.
measure.guide4: There is no right or wrong writing. This record is not saved.
measure.factsEmpty: Write at least one line in the facts box.
measure.factsTitle: Now sort this record into three boxes.
measure.factDef: A fact is what a camera and a recorder would capture — what can be stated with specific names and numbers.
measure.delusionDef: A delusion is what gets layered on top — judgment, evaluation, guess, imagination. A delusion is neither good nor bad. It is an indicator of how you interpret the world. Delusion needs no box — what remains after the three boxes is the delusion.
measure.example1: "I can't buy it because I have no money" is a delusion. The fact: "My wallet has 0 dollars in it."
measure.example2: "I stayed at a nice hotel" is a delusion. The fact: "I stayed at the Ritz-Carlton" — 'nice' is a thought you added.
measure.factsHelp: Only what a camera would catch — numbers, times, words heard, actions seen. If an adjective slips in, it is no longer a fact.
measure.feelingsHelp: What rose inside you in that moment. A feeling is a signal (chest tightening, heat rising); an emotion is a response (hurt, anxious) — both belong here. Nothing here is right or wrong. But 'feeling ignored' is not a feeling — once the other person's intent enters, it is already an interpretation.
measure.actionsHelp: What you actually did or how you reacted. What you said, how you moved.
measure.toMirror: Hold it up to the mirror
measure.resultTitle: The mirror looked at your facts box.
measure.clean: Every sentence here would be caught on camera.
measure.unclear: Which side would you place this on?
measure.mirrorRatio: The mirror splits it as fact {f} : delusion {d}.
measure.limit: That's all for today. You can mirror again tomorrow.
measure.recallNoAnswer: Yesterday's question was left unanswered. On to today's record.

save.prompt: This question does not end here.
save.hint: Get an Ozero ID — tomorrow, at the time you choose, today's answer comes back with the mirror's reading of it. Keep three days and the mirror reflects what repeats. No address, no password.
save.existing: I already have an Ozero ID
save.doneTime: Tomorrow at {t}, today's answer comes back with its mirror.
save.savedToday: Today's mirror is kept. Tomorrow your answer comes back with its mirror.
save.codeInvalid: That ID format is not right. Example: o023
save.codeTaken: That ID is already registered. If you're new, get a fresh ID.
save.failed: Could not save. Please try again.

me.title: My mirror
me.none: You don't have an Ozero ID yet.
me.deliverAt: The mirror opens at {t} each day.

today.building: Today's dial — built in the next step.
